name: Route of the Lakes: Balaguer Loop
name_original: Colinas del Segrià - Vuelta circular desde Balaguer
description: This easy 26.4-mile (42.5 km) road cycling route from Balaguer offers 751 feet (229 metres) of elevation gain through the Segrià hills.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Balaguer traverse a diverse landscape, situated between the plains and mountains of Lleida and alongside the Segre River. The region features varied terrain, from relatively flat riverbank paths to rolling hills and more challenging mountain ascents. Cyclists encounter mostly paved surfaces, with routes often leading to picturesque reservoirs and through the scenic Noguera region. This blend of natural features provides options for various fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Balaguer The most popular road cycling route is To the Camarassa reservoir - Circular via the Serra de Montclús, a 53.9 miles (86.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 31 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through the Serra de Montclús. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Circular around the region of la Noguera, a moderate 31.1 miles (50.0 km) path. This route provides varied terrain and scenic views through the rolling hills of the Noguera region. Local road cyclists also love the Route of the Lakes: Balaguer Loop, a 26.4 miles (42.5 km) trail leading through the Segrià hills, often completed in about 1 hour 56 minutes. Road cycling around Balaguer is defined by circuits along the Segre River, routes to various reservoirs, and rides through the rolling hills of La Noguera. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging, longer routes. The routes in Balaguer are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Balaguer's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Balaguer?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Balaguer, offering a diverse range of experiences. This includes 5 easy routes, 34 moderate routes, and 16 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Balaguer?
A: Yes, Balaguer offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942450733/riverbank-of-the-segre-river-circular-route-from-the-city-of-balaguer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverbank of the Segre River - Circular route from the city of Balaguer&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a gentle ride along the river with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, Balaguer offers routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942481261/to-the-camarassa-reservoir-circular-via-the-serra-de-montclus" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;To the Camarassa reservoir - Circular via the Serra de Montclús&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 86.7 km route with substantial climbing through the Serra de Montclús. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31490612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balaguer Bridge – Port d'Àger loop from Balaguer&lt;/a&gt;, which also features considerable elevation changes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the Balaguer area?
A: Many road cycling routes around Balaguer are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942472104/circular-around-the-region-of-la-noguera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular around the region of la Noguera&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 50 km loop offering varied terrain and scenic views. Another excellent circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944694858/route-of-the-lakes-balaguer-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Lakes: Balaguer Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 42.5 km ride through the Segrià hills.

Q: What scenic attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Balaguer's road cycling routes often pass by picturesque natural features and historical sites. You can cycle along the Segre River, visit various reservoirs like Camarasa, Canelles, and Sant Llorenç de Montgai, or explore the rolling hills of La Noguera. The region also features historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3062857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alòs de Balaguer Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1042597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jordi Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Balaguer?
A: While many routes are designed for road bikes, families with older children comfortable with road cycling can enjoy the easier, flatter sections, particularly those along the Segre River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942450733/riverbank-of-the-segre-river-circular-route-from-the-city-of-balaguer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverbank of the Segre River - Circular route from the city of Balaguer&lt;/a&gt; is a good starting point for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Balaguer?
A: The diverse climate of Balaguer makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Summer can be warm, especially in the plains, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter cycling is possible, but check local weather conditions for cooler temperatures and potential rain.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Balaguer?
A: Balaguer, as a regional hub, offers various parking options within the city, particularly near the Segre River or in the town center, from where many routes originate or are easily accessible. Specific parking details for individual trailheads are often available on the respective route pages.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Balaguer?
A: The road cycling experience in Balaguer is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Cyclists often praise the region's varied terrain, from flat river paths to challenging hilly landscapes, and the scenic beauty of the reservoirs and the Noguera region. The well-maintained paved surfaces and the blend of natural and historical sights are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Many road cycling routes in Balaguer pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find local cafes, bakeries, or restaurants for refreshments. Routes leading to reservoirs or through more populated areas of La Noguera will offer more opportunities for stops. It's always a good idea to check the route details or a map beforehand to plan your refreshment breaks.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other outdoor activities in Balaguer?
A: Absolutely. Balaguer is an excellent base for combining road cycling with other outdoor activities. The reservoirs (Camarasa, Canelles, Santa Anna, Sant Llorenç de Montgai) are popular for water sports like kayaking and canoeing. The Montsec-Montsec routes also offer access to areas known for hiking and exploring natural features like the Mont-rebei Gorge, though these might require a separate trip or specific planning.
tips: The church of Santa Maria de Balaguer dates from the 14th century. It has spectacular views of the city of Balaguer.
Albesa is a town and municipality in Catalonia located in the region of La Noguera. The town is located to the left of the Noguera Ribagorçana shortly before its mouth in the Segre.

Emblematic monastery from the 12th century, built at a time of repopulation of territories recovered by the Christians. Balaguer was conquered in 1105, but it was not until the middle of the 12th century that repopulation policies began.

faqs.question: Where does the Colinas del Segrià - Circular route from Balaguer start and is there parking?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends in Balaguer. While specific parking details for the trailhead aren't provided, Balaguer, as a hub for cycling events, generally offers various parking options within the town. You can typically find street parking or designated lots in urban areas.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily traverses the Lleida Plain, offering generally flat to rolling terrain. You can expect mostly paved surfaces, which are well-suited for racebiking. While the broader region can feature more challenging climbs towards the Pre-Pyrenees, this specific route is rated 'easy' with a moderate elevation gain, suggesting a smooth ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the rich agricultural heartland of the Lleida Plain, characterized by wide open fields, fruit orchards, and cereal crops. The route will likely pass through charming villages and offer views of historical sites scattered across the horizon, providing a pleasant and scenic ride.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the Colinas del Segrià route?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the impressive Church of Santa Maria de Balaguer, the settlement of Albesa, and the historic Cistercian Monastery of Santa María de les Franqueses.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is rated as 'easy' and covers approximately 42.5 km with a modest elevation gain of around 229 meters. Its flat to rolling terrain makes it a good option for beginners or those looking for a relaxed racebike ride.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Lleida region, including the Segrià comarca, is known for its pleasant cycling conditions. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling. Summers can be hot, while winters are cooler but often still rideable.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: As this is a racebike route primarily on paved roads and through agricultural areas, it's generally not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists. If you're looking for dog-friendly outdoor activities, consider walking or hiking trails where dogs are explicitly permitted and can be safely managed.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths, making it freely accessible to all cyclists.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Ruta dels Llacs (26%), Camino de Santiago por Huesca (8%), and El Camí IT2TR1 El Camí de Ponent: de Balaguer a Montserrat (8%). You'll also find smaller segments of other local trails like Camí del Braçal de Lleida and Camí d'Albesa.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: While specific cafes aren't listed, the route passes through the town of Balaguer and the settlement of Albesa. These locations are likely to have amenities such as cafes, shops, or restaurants where you can stop for refreshments, especially in Balaguer at the start and end of your ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 56 minutes to complete on a racebike. This duration is for continuous cycling and does not include stops for sightseeing or refreshments.
